factors.summary: The company faces potential financial or operational impacts from litigation and legal claims, including product liability and environmental issues.

evidence.text: We are involved in various claims and lawsuits incidental to our business, including those involving product liability, labor relations, alleged exposure to asbestos-containing materials and environmental matters, some of which claim significant damages.
evidence.subsection: We are Subject to Litigation and Tax, Environmental and Other Regulations that Could Have an Adverse Effect on Our Results of Operations.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces potential financial or operational impacts from various claims and lawsuits, which could pose a moderate risk.

factors.summary: Free cash flow has increased, primarily due to higher net income and lower working capital.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities increased $170 million to $496 million in 2018 compared to $325 million in 2017. This increase was primarily attributable to the decrease in working capital and an increase in net income.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow has increased due to higher net income and lower working capital, indicating strong cash generation.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are expected to increase, focusing on manufacturing expansion and technology investments.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures in 2018 were primarily related to expansion of our manufacturing capacity and equipment and investments in systems and software to support the overall enterprise. We expect capital expenditures of approximately $215 million in 2019, including $115 million to complete the reconstruction of the Marshalltown, Iowa manufacturing facility.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expected increase in capital expenditures for manufacturing expansion and technology investments suggests growth opportunities.

factors.summary: Debt leverage has increased, primarily due to a decrease in equity.

evidence.text: Our debt-to-total-capital ratio increased to 116.8% at December 31, 2018 compared to 95.2% at December 31, 2017. The increase in the ratio in 2018 is primarily due to the decrease in equity.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Debt leverage has increased due to a decrease in equity, raising concerns about financial stability.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are increasing through higher dividends and share repurchases.

evidence.text: Dividend payments were $94 million in 2018 compared to $80 million in 2017, with the increase due primarily to the increase in dividends approved by the Board of Directors. We returned $450 million to our investors through share repurchases in 2018 and expect to return another $350 million in 2019.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Higher dividends and increased share repurchases indicate strong returns to shareholders, reflecting positive capital allocation.

factors.summary: Order backlog is not explicitly mentioned, but there is evidence of production shifting and insurance recoveries affecting operations.

evidence.text: We have shifted production of certain products that were previously only produced at our Marshalltown facility to our other facilities. We believe this provides increased manufacturing flexibility.
evidence.subsection: Overview of Results

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No explicit mention of order backlog, so sentiment is neutral due to insufficient information.

factors.summary: The company is holding prices despite some margin compression.

evidence.text: We saw margin decreases of 120 bps from higher commodity costs, 100 bps from higher freight and distribution costs, and 50 bps from other product costs. These decreases were offset by increases of 130 bps from favorable price and mix and 70 bps from sourcing and engineering-led cost reductions.
evidence.subsection: Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is holding prices despite margin compression, showing resilience in maintaining revenue.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments due to the sale of international businesses.

evidence.text: The loss of sales from the divested Australia, Asia and South America businesses contributed 16% and price and mix combined was 1% lower partially offset by sales volume increases of 1% and 1% from foreign currency exchange rates.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2018 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2017 - Results by Segment

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments due to the sale of international businesses, which could negatively impact profitability.

factors.summary: Residential and Commercial segments are growing, while Refrigeration is declining due to divestitures.

evidence.text: Despite the impact of the tornado at our Marshalltown facility, the Residential Heating & Cooling segment performed well in 2018, with a 4.0% increase in net sales and a $26 million increase in segment profit compared to 2017. Our Commercial Heating & Cooling segment also performed well in 2018 with a 7.2% increase in net sales and a $2 million increase in segment profit compared to 2017. This segment’s results were driven by volume and price gains. Sales in our Refrigeration segment decreased 15% and segment profit decreased $7 million compared to 2017 mostly due to the sale of our Australia, Asia and South America businesses.
evidence.subsection: Overview of Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Residential and Commercial segments are growing, indicating positive market momentum despite Refrigeration's decline.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable as the company has not mentioned significant wage inflation or benefit expenses impacting margins.

evidence.text: The principal elements of cost of goods sold are components, raw materials, factory overhead, labor, estimated costs of warranty expense and freight and distribution costs. [...] We believe this provides increased manufacturing flexibility.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are manageable without significant wage inflation or benefit expenses.

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives are delivering savings, with restructuring charges slightly decreasing year-over-year.

evidence.text: Restructuring charges were $3.0 million in 2018 compared to $3.2 million in 2017. The charges in 2018 and 2017 were primarily for projects to realign resources and enhance manufacturing and distribution capabilities.
evidence.subsection: Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction initiatives are delivering savings with slight decrease in restructuring charges year-over-year.

factors.summary: Productivity improvements are being made through production shifts and efficiency gains, though disrupted by the tornado incident.

evidence.text: We have shifted production of certain products that were previously only produced at our Marshalltown facility to our other facilities. [...] These declines were partially offset by favorable price and mix and sourcing and engineering-led cost reductions across our business.
evidence.subsection: Overview of Results

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Productivity improvements are being made but disrupted by the tornado incident.

factors.summary: Supply chain is disrupted due to the tornado incident, but production has been shifted to other facilities.

evidence.text: We have shifted production of certain products that were previously only produced at our Marshalltown facility to our other facilities. [...] We believe this provides increased manufacturing flexibility.
evidence.subsection: Overview of Results

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Supply chain disruption from the tornado but production shifted to other facilities.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are being managed, with some impact from the tornado incident leading to freight adjustments.

evidence.text: We incurred expenses of $86.0 million related to damages caused by the tornado, which included the write-off of damaged property, equipment and inventory, freight to move product to other warehouses and professional fees to secure and maintain the site.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Inventory levels are being managed but impacted by tornado-related freight adjustments.

factors.summary: Management has not explicitly provided forward guidance in the text.

evidence.text: No specific forward guidance is mentioned in the provided text.
evidence.subsection: Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No specific forward guidance provided in the text.

factors.summary: Management appears cautious due to challenges from the tornado and economic factors but is taking actions to mitigate risks.

evidence.text: We believe this provides increased manufacturing flexibility. [...] We expect to receive approximately $83 million of insurance proceeds related to lost profits and $149 million of insurance proceeds to fund the reconstruction of the facility and as reimbursement for other losses and expenses expected to be incurred.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Management is cautious due to tornado and economic challenges but taking mitigating actions.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy is focused on cost reduction, efficiency, and market expansion, though some international segments were divested.

evidence.text: We believe this provides increased manufacturing flexibility. [...] We expect our effective tax rate will be between 22% and 23% in future years excluding the impact of excess tax benefits.
evidence.subsection: Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Focus on cost reduction, efficiency, and market expansion indicates a strategic positive outlook.

factors.summary: Insurance recoveries and cost reduction initiatives are key catalysts for improving results.

evidence.text: We have received insurance recoveries of $124.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2018. [...] These declines were partially offset by favorable price and mix and sourcing and engineering-led cost reductions across our business.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Insurance recoveries and cost reduction initiatives are catalysts for improving results.

factors.summary: Construction activity is a factor in demand, particularly in new construction business.

evidence.text: The demand for our products and services is seasonal and significantly impacted by the weather. [...] The balance comprised of new construction business.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Construction activity impacts demand, but no explicit mention of inventory levels or future spending trends.

factors.summary: Replacement cycles are active, driven by seasonal weather and equipment age.

evidence.text: The demand for our products and services is seasonal and significantly impacted by the weather. [...] Warmer than normal summer temperatures generate demand for replacement air conditioning and refrigeration products and services, and colder than normal winter temperatures have a similar effect on heating products and services.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Replacement cycles are active, but driven by seasonal factors without clear positive or negative trend.

factors.summary: Dealer inventory levels are not explicitly mentioned, but distribution and dealer activity is part of the sales strategy.

evidence.text: We sell our products and services through a combination of direct sales, distributors and company-owned stores.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Distribution strategy mentioned, but no specific sentiment on dealer inventory levels.

factors.summary: Aftermarket service revenue is not explicitly discussed, but it is part of the overall business segments.

evidence.text: We sell our products and services through a combination of direct sales, distributors and company-owned stores. [...] The demand for our products and services is seasonal and significantly impacted by the weather.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Aftermarket service is part of business segments, but no explicit sentiment expressed.

factors.summary: Interest rate changes are affecting the company through tax benefits and borrowing costs.

evidence.text: The income tax provision was $108 million in 2018 compared to $157 million in 2017, and the effective tax rate was 23% in 2018 compared to 34% in 2017. [...] Net interest expense of $38 million in 2018 increased from $31 million in 2017 primarily due to an increase in our average borrowings and rising interest rates.
evidence.subsection: Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Impact of rising interest rates on borrowing costs and tax benefits suggests a negative sentiment.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are impacting results, particularly due to weakening rates.

evidence.text: Foreign currency exchange losses increased in 2018 primarily due to weakening in foreign exchange rates in our primary markets.
evidence.subsection: Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Weakening foreign exchange rates are negatively impacting results.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks are not explicitly mentioned, but foreign exchange and trade factors are noted.

evidence.text: Foreign currency exchange losses increased in 2018 primarily due to weakening in foreign exchange rates in our primary markets. [...] The contractor tax payments relate to a charge for underpaid contractor taxes at one of our non-U.S. subsidiaries.
evidence.subsection: Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Geopolitical risks are not explicitly mentioned, but foreign exchange and trade factors are noted, which could pose risks.

factors.summary: The company faces near-term risks related to security breaches and disruptions in its information systems, which could harm its reputation, increase costs, or lead to loss of revenue. Additionally, there are risks related to continued impact from weather events, economic conditions, and geopolitical factors.

evidence.text: Our information systems and those of our business partners are important to our business activities... These information systems have been, and will likely continue to be, subject to attack. While we have implemented controls and taken other preventative actions to strengthen these systems against future attacks, we can give no assurance that these controls and preventative actions will be effective. Any breach of data security could result in a disruption of our services or improper disclosure of personal data or confidential information, which could harm our reputation, require us to expend resources to remedy such a security breach or defend against further attacks or subject us to liability under laws that protect personal data, resulting in increased operating costs or loss of revenue.
evidence.subsection: Security Breaches and Other Disruptions or Misuse of Information Systems We Rely Upon Could Affect Our Ability to Conduct Our Business Effectively.

evidence.text: The demand for our products and services is seasonal and significantly impacted by the weather. [...] Foreign currency exchange losses increased in 2018 primarily due to weakening in foreign exchange rates in our primary markets.
evidence.subsection: Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include security breaches, weather events, and economic conditions, which could harm the company's reputation and financials.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing rising input costs, particularly for steel, copper, and aluminum, which are creating margin pressure. While they are partially offsetting these increases through various measures like pricing actions, contracts, futures, and cost reduction initiatives, there remains a risk of supply chain disruptions or further cost escalations, which could negatively impact their cash flow or results of operations.

evidence.text: The principal raw materials used in our manufacturing processes are steel, copper and aluminum. In recent years, pricing volatility for these commodities and related components has impacted us and the HVACR industry in general. We seek to mitigate the impact of commodity price volatility through a combination of pricing actions, vendor contracts, improved production efficiency and cost reduction initiatives. We also partially mitigate volatility in the prices of these commodities by entering into futures contracts and fixed forward contracts.
evidence.subsection: Price Volatility for Commodities and Components We Purchase or Significant Supply Interruptions Could Have an Adverse Effect on Our Cash Flow or Results of Operations.

evidence.text: Higher commodity costs impacted segment profit, with $20 million in higher commodity costs.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2017 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2016 - Results by Segment

evidence.text: We depend on raw materials, such as steel, copper and aluminum, and components purchased from third parties to manufacture our products. If a supplier is unable or unwilling to meet our supply requirements, including suffering any disruptions at its facilities or in its supply chain, we could experience supply interruptions or cost increases, either of which could have an adverse effect on our results of operations.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising input costs for steel, copper, and aluminum are creating margin pressure despite mitigation efforts.

factors.summary: The company's margins are under mixed pressure, with cost increases from raw materials and commodities negatively impacting margins, partially offset by cost reduction initiatives and favorable pricing strategies.

evidence.text: An increase in raw materials prices not covered by our fixed price arrangements could significantly increase our cost of goods sold and negatively impact our margins if we are unable to effectively pass such price increases on to our customers.
evidence.subsection: Price Volatility for Commodities and Components We Purchase or Significant Supply Interruptions Could Have an Adverse Effect on Our Cash Flow or Results of Operations.

evidence.text: Gross profit margins for 2018 decreased 70 basis points ('bps') to 28.6% compared to 29.3% in 2017. We saw margin decreases of 120 bps from higher commodity costs, 100 bps from higher freight and distribution costs, and 50 bps from other product costs. These decreases were offset by increases of 130 bps from favorable price and mix and 70 bps from sourcing and engineering-led cost reductions.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2017 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2016 - Results by Segment

evidence.text: Segment profit in 2017 increased $25 million due to ... $21 million from sourcing and engineering-led cost reductions, $15 million from favorable price, ... Segment profit in 2017 increased $8 million compared to 2016 due to ... $7 million from sourcing and engineering-led cost reductions, ... Segment profit in 2017 increased $4 million compared to 2016 due to ... $9 million from sourcing and engineering-led cost reductions.
evidence.subsection: Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Margins are under pressure due to cost increases, though partially offset by initiatives, indicating mixed but overall negative trajectory.

factors.summary: The company faces significant key sensitivities across multiple areas, including intellectual property risks, foreign exchange rates, commodity prices, and interest rates. These factors could lead to increased costs, operational disruptions, or financial impacts on net income and effective tax rates.

evidence.text: Our intellectual property rights could be challenged, invalidated, infringed, circumvented, or be insufficient to permit us to take advantage of current market trends or to otherwise provide competitive advantages. Further, the laws of certain countries do not protect proprietary rights to the same extent as the laws of the United States. Third parties may also claim that we are infringing upon their intellectual property rights. If we do not license infringed intellectual property or if we are required to substitute similar technology from another source, our operations could be adversely affected.
evidence.subsection: Security Breaches and Other Disruptions or Misuse of Information Systems We Rely Upon Could Affect Our Ability to Conduct Our Business Effectively.

evidence.text: A 10% change in foreign exchange rates would have an estimated $2.1 million, $5.2 million and $4.0 million impact to net income for the years ended December 31, 2018, 2017 and 2016, respectively. Fluctuations in metal commodity prices impact the value of the futures contracts that we hold.
evidence.subsection: Market Risk

evidence.text: The principal raw materials used in our manufacturing processes are steel, copper and aluminum.
evidence.subsection: Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

evidence.text: The income tax provision was $108 million in 2018 compared to $157 million in 2017, and the effective tax rate was 23% in 2018 compared to 34% in 2017.
evidence.subsection: Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant sensitivities to various factors like IP, FX, commodities, and interest rates pose risks to net income and tax rates.

factors.summary: The company's capacity utilization is likely stable or increased due to production shifts post-tornado, though there are risks related to single-location production facilities and reliance on key suppliers which could negatively impact their ability to meet customer demand.

evidence.text: We have shifted production of certain products that were previously only produced at our Marshalltown facility to our other facilities. [...] We believe this provides increased manufacturing flexibility.
evidence.subsection: Our Ability to Meet Customer Demand may be Limited by Our Single-Location Production Facilities, Reliance on Certain Key Suppliers and Unanticipated Significant Shifts in Customer Demand.

evidence.text: We manufacture many of our products at single-location production facilities, and we rely on certain suppliers who also may concentrate production in single locations. Any significant interruptions in production at one or more of our facilities, or at a facility of one of our suppliers, could negatively impact our ability to deliver our products to our customers.
evidence.subsection: Overview of Results

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Capacity utilization is stable or increased post-tornado, but risks from single-location production and suppliers exist.

factors.summary: Economic conditions, particularly in the U.S. construction industry, are a significant factor affecting the company's financial performance. While the industry has shown improvement, future sales performance is uncertain and may not sustain or exceed previous improvements. Additionally, economic conditions are influenced by various factors such as interest rates, financing availability, population trends, new construction, and consumer spending, which can have both positive and negative impacts on demand for the company's products and services.

evidence.text: Our business is affected by the performance of the U.S. construction industry. Although the industry has improved over the last several years, our sales may not continue to improve or such improvement may be limited or lower than expected.
evidence.subsection: Our Financial Performance Is Affected by the Conditions of the U.S. Construction Industry.

evidence.text: Demand for our products and services is influenced by national and regional economic and demographic factors, such as interest rates, the availability of financing, regional population and employment trends, new construction, general economic conditions and consumer spending habits and confidence.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed impact from economic conditions with some improvement in construction but uncertain future performance.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, showing growth in certain segments but also exhibiting seasonal variations and economic sensitivity.

evidence.text: Sales volume increased by 7% primarily due to market growth. Sales volume increased by 5% primarily due to market growth and 1% from favorable foreign currency.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2017 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2016 - Results by Segment

evidence.text: The demand for our products and services is seasonal and significantly impacted by the weather. Warmer than normal summer temperatures generate demand for replacement air conditioning and refrigeration products and services, and colder than normal winter temperatures have a similar effect on heating products and services.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Demand shows growth in some areas but is seasonal and economically sensitive, leading to mixed signals.

factors.summary: The company faces potential risks related to quality and safety, primarily through warranty costs and product liability claims, which could negatively impact its financial performance. While no significant incidents are mentioned, warranty expenses are a component of cost of goods sold, indicating ongoing management of these risks.

evidence.text: Warranties of such extended lengths pose a risk to us as actual future costs may exceed our current estimates of those costs. Warranty expense is recorded on the date that revenue is recognized and requires significant assumptions about what costs will be incurred in the future. We may be required to record material adjustments to accruals and expense in the future if actual costs for these warranties are different from our assumptions.
evidence.subsection: We May Incur Substantial Costs as a Result of Claims Which Could Have an Adverse Effect on Our Results of Operations.

evidence.text: The principal elements of cost of goods sold are components, raw materials, factory overhead, labor, estimated costs of warranty expense and freight and distribution costs.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces potential risks related to quality and safety, with ongoing warranty expenses that could negatively impact financial performance.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are consistently increasing across all segments, driven by market growth and expansion. The Residential and Commercial segments particularly show strong growth with increases in both sales volume and price/mix contributions.

evidence.text: Sales volume increased by 7% primarily due to market growth. Sales volume increased by 5% primarily due to market growth and 1% from favorable foreign currency.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2017 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2016 - Results by Segment

evidence.text: Residential Heating & Cooling net sales increased 4% in 2018 compared to 2017. Sales volume increased 2% and price and mix combined increased 2%. Commercial Heating & Cooling net sales increased 7% in 2018 compared to 2017. Sales volume increased 5% and price and mix combined increased 2%.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2018 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2017 - Results by Segment

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are consistently increasing across all segments, driven by market growth and expansion, indicating strong demand.

factors.summary: The company experienced mixed organic growth across its segments. While the Residential and Commercial segments saw slight increases, the Refrigeration segment faced declines due to business sales.

evidence.text: Net sales in 2017 were flat compared to 2016. Favorable foreign currency increased sales by 1% offset by lower sales volume of 1% primarily from our North America supermarket business.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2017 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2016 - Results by Segment

evidence.text: Net sales increased 1.2% in 2018 compared to 2017, primarily driven by volume and price increases. The increase in volume was primarily due to market growth in our Residential Heating and Cooling and Commercial Heating and Cooling segments. These increases were partially offset by the impact due to the sale of our Australia, Asia and South America businesses in our Refrigeration segment.
evidence.subsection: Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company experienced mixed organic growth across segments, with some increases and declines, resulting in an overall mixed sentiment.
